name: Thermodynamic Governance Framework
description: Physics-based analysis of governance and economic systems. Energy accounting before narratives. Enforcement as failure indicator. The collapse equation.

collapse_equation.physics_basis: Imposing identical rules across varying energy landscapes creates systematic inefficiency. A CUBE applied outside its first-principles scope becomes a cage.

rosetta_mapping.distance: SHAPE.CUBE scope violation — containment without contact
rosetta_mapping.comfort: SHAPE.DODECA sensor suppression — intuition and trust overridden
rosetta_mapping.authoritarian_rule: CORDYCEPS.FORCE_SINGLE_SHAPE — one shape forced on all

enforcement_thermodynamics.principle: Enforcement requirements prove energy deficit between rules and local reality
enforcement_thermodynamics.voluntary_compliance: Rules aligned with thermodynamic constraints — minimal enforcement needed
enforcement_thermodynamics.forced_compliance: Rules violate thermodynamic constraints — enforcement cost escalates
enforcement_thermodynamics.feedback_loop: More enforcement → costs accelerate deficit → production drops (energy diverted to enforcement) → more enforcement needed
enforcement_thermodynamics.rosetta_axiom: ENERGY_CONSERVATION — energy spent on enforcement is energy not spent on production

hidden_labor.principle: Care work, domestic maintenance, emotional support are critical energy flows
hidden_labor.measurement_gap: GDP and standard metrics exclude these flows
hidden_labor.implication: Disconnecting political voice from high-energy contributors creates systemic fragility

semantic_paradoxes.name: Dividend Income Paradox
semantic_paradoxes.structure: Living entirely from investment returns = structurally identical to receiving redistribution without contribution
semantic_paradoxes.shadow: Same structure, opposite labels based on who benefits

semantic_paradoxes.name: Collective Ownership Paradox
semantic_paradoxes.structure: Shareholders collectively owning corporations = capitalist. Workers collectively owning workplaces = socialist.

semantic_paradoxes.name: Risk Asymmetry Paradox
semantic_paradoxes.structure: Investors claim higher risk justifies higher returns, but risk is diversified and subsidized (bailouts, limited liability). Workers bear concentrated, unprotected risk.
semantic_paradoxes.shadow: Risk is real but distributed inversely to the narrative

semantic_paradoxes.name: Infrastructure Paradox
semantic_paradoxes.structure: Every business relies on publicly-funded infrastructure. Genuine independence impossible at scale.
semantic_paradoxes.shadow: The CUBE is collective. Pretending it isn't is a scope violation.

energy_accounting_method.step_1: Calculate energy inputs (labor, infrastructure, materials, hidden work)
energy_accounting_method.step_2: Calculate energy outputs (goods, services, waste, maintenance)
energy_accounting_method.step_3: Calculate work requirements (what the system needs to sustain itself)
energy_accounting_method.step_5: When capacity < requirements, system is unsustainable regardless of narrative
energy_accounting_method.rosetta_parallel: This is compute_seed_state() applied to economic systems
